steps.title: Check if the equations are equivalent
steps.content: The second equation is $24x + 21y = 27$. Divide by 3: $$\frac{24x + 21y}{3} = \frac{27}{3}$$ This simplifies to $8x + 7y = 9$, which is exactly the first equation. Both equations represent the same line—there are infinitely many solutions.

steps.title: Solve for the parametric form of points on the line
steps.content: Define $L(x,y)=8x+7y$. A correct parametrization must make $L(x,y)=9$ for every value of $r$. Set $r=[0,1]$ and evaluate each choice. Choice A returns $[9,9]$, so it works for the whole line.

steps.title: Confirm choice A
steps.content: For choice A, $x=r$ and $y=-\frac{8r}{7}+\frac{9}{7}$. Substitution gives $8r+7\left(-\frac{8r}{7}+\frac{9}{7}\right)=8r-8r+9=9$, true for every $r$. Choice A is correct.
